entities.name: MTP2 PROTEIN
entities.description: A protein whose spatio-temporal and phytohormonal expression patterns and characterization are studied across different plant species

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: An essential micronutrient involved in physiological processes of plants, available in oxidized form Zn2+

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: The biological organisms in which zinc uptake, toxicity, and MTP2 protein expression are analyzed

entities.name: ZINC TOXICITY
entities.description: The toxic effects caused by an excess accumulation of zinc in plants

entities.name: IRON DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition that induces the uptake of zinc, leading to its excess accumulation in the plant cytoplasm

entities.name: VACUOLES
entities.description: Organelles that act as the main storage and detoxifying sites for excess zinc in the cytoplasm

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium from which plants obtain zinc through mass flow and diffusion

entities.name: LOW ACIDIC PH
entities.description: A soil condition that increases the availability of zinc to plants

entities.name: HIGH PH
entities.description: A soil condition that reduces the availability of zinc to plants

entities.name: ROOT PLASMA MEMBRANE TRANSPORTERS
entities.description: Proteins in the root plasma membrane responsible for the major uptake of zinc from the soil

relationships.description: leads to accumulation of excess zinc
